briefdescription.para: Initialize the Device Config Interface driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called before other functions of the driver are called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the config structure.
is the base address for the device. It could be a virtual address if address translation is supported in the system, otherwise it is the physical address.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started.

simplesect.para:
The very first APB access to the Device Configuration Interface block needs to be a write to the UNLOCK register with the value of 0x757BDF0D. This step is to be done once after reset, any other APB access has to come after this. The APB access is considered illegal if the step is not done or if it is done incorrectly. Furthermore, if any of efuse_sec_cfg[5:0] is high, the following additional actions would be carried out. In other words, if all bits are low, the following steps are not done.
AES is disabled
All APB writes disabled
SoC debug fully enabled

briefdescription.para: The functions enables the PCAP interface by setting the PCAP mode bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: Enable FPGA programming from PCAP interface. Enabling this bit disables all the external interfaces from programming of FPGA except for ICAP. The user needs to ensure that the FPGA is programmed through either PCAP or ICAP.

briefdescription.para: The functions disables the PCAP interface by clearing the PCAP mode bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit mask data to be written to the Register. The mask definitions are defined in the xdevcfg_hw.h file.

briefdescription.para: The function Clears the specified bit positions of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit value which holds the bit positions to be cleared.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Control Register. Use the XDCFG_CTRL_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Lock Register.

detaileddescription.para: These bits can only be set to a 1. They will be cleared after a Power On Reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Lock Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Lock Register. Use the XDCFG_CR_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Configuration Register with the given value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Configuration Register with the given value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Config Register. Use the XDCFG_CFG_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Status Register. Use the XDCFG_STATUS_*_MASK constants defined in xdevcfg_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the contents of the ROM Shadow Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

simplesect.para: This register is can only be written and is used to control the RAM shadow of 32 bit 4K page ROM pages in user mode

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Software ID Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: This register is locked for write once the system enters usermode. Hence API for reading the register only is provided.

briefdescription.para: The function sets the bit mask for the feature in Miscellaneous Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
is the bit-mask of the feature to be set.

briefdescription.para: The function reads the contents of the Miscellaneous Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: This register is locked for write once the system enters usermode. Hence API to reading the register only is provided.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if DMA command queue is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS is the DMA is busy XST_FAILURE if the DMA is idle
The DMA queue has a depth of two.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, u32 SourcePtr, u32 DestPtr, u32 SrcWordLength, u32 DestWordLength)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_InitiateDma

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the DMA transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the caller function to ensure that correct values are passed to this function. The 2 LSBs of the SourcePtr (Source)/ DestPtr (Destination) address when equal to 2�b01 indicates the last DMA command of an overall transfer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, u32 SourcePtr, u32 SrcWordLength, u32 DestPtr, u32 DestWordLength)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_PcapReadback

briefdescription.para: This function Implements the DMA Read Command.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to transfer the image data from FPGA to the external memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.

simplesect.para:
- XST_INVALID_PARAM if source address/length is invalid.
XST_SUCCESS if DMA transfer initiated properly.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDcfg *InstancePtr, void *SourcePtr, u32 SrcWordLength, void *DestPtr, u32 DestWordLength, u32 TransferType)
memberdef.name: XDcfg_Transfer

briefdescription.para: This function starts the DMA transfer.

detaileddescription.para: This function only starts the operation and returns before the operation may be completed. If the interrupt is enabled, an interrupt will be generated when the operation is completed, otherwise it is necessary to poll the Status register to determine when it is completed. It is the responsibility of the caller to determine when the operation is completed by handling the generated interrupt or polling the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDcfg instance.
contains a pointer to the source memory where the data is to be transferred from.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the source transfer.
contains a pointer to the destination memory where the data is to be transferred to.
is the number of words (32 bit) to be transferred for the Destination transfer.
contains the type of PCAP transfer being requested. The definitions can be found in the xdevcfg.h file.

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM if invalid Source / Destination address is sent or an invalid Source / Destination length is sent

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the caller to ensure that the cache is flushed and invalidated both before the DMA operation is started and after the DMA operation completes if the memory pointed to is cached. The caller must also ensure that the pointers contain physical address rather than a virtual address if address translation is being used. The 2 LSBs of the SourcePtr (Source)/ DestPtr (Destination) address when equal to 2�b01 indicates the last DMA command of an overall transfer.
